headline: Polio Vaccine Declared Safe After Nationwide Trials
deck: Dr. Salk's miracle is here. Parents are lining up around the block.
article:
In a development that has stunned observers across the nation, polio vaccine declared safe after nationwide trials. Sources close to the matter have confirmed that the situation remains fluid.

Officials in the central district have declined to comment, but witnesses describe a scene of both confusion and cautious optimism. The implications for the average citizen remain unclear.

Historical records suggest a similar event occurred nearly a century ago in this timeline, though on a much smaller scale. International observers have expressed both admiration and concern.

Residents of the capital expressed mixed reactions. 'I never thought I'd see the day,' said one local merchant. 'It changes everything, and yet it changes nothing.'

Only time will tell whether this marks the beginning of a new era or merely a footnote in the annals of this parallel history.
author: Special Reporter

oped.title: Why Every American Family Needs a Bomb Shelter
oped.author: A Concerned Father
oped.body: It is not paranoia. It is prudence. When the sirens sound, you will not have time to dig. You will have time to thank yourself.

classifieds.text: 1953 Chevrolet Bel Air. Two-tone. Low miles. The American dream on four wheels.
Salesman wanted for vacuum demonstration. Must own car and smile.
Fallout shelter construction. Family rates. Government approved.

comic.caption: 'The future is here, and it is slightly greasy.'
